# Singularity Diagnostics Matrix

| Paper | Construction | Entanglement Role | Singular Region Claim | Boundary Observable Access | Main Open Issue |
| ----- | ------------ | ----------------- | --------------------- | -------------------------- | --------------- |
| arXiv:1810.10601 | holographic microstate with ETW-like interior FRW region | interior probe strength tied to entanglement entropy of large subsystems | cosmological region exists behind the horizon; singularity fate not fully resolved | large-subsystem entanglement entropy is explicitly proposed as a probe | does the probe distinguish a resolved crunch from an encoded one? |
| arXiv:2102.05057 | confinement-inspired Euclidean construction continued to cosmology or wormhole | entanglement is implicit in the joined geometry and IR flow | proposes a class of big-bang/big-crunch cosmologies in holography | indirect; main evidence is geometric and field-theoretic construction | boundary observable dictionary is less explicit than in later work |
| arXiv:2206.14821 | accelerating cosmology from a holographic wormhole | random or structured entanglement between CFT sectors supports cosmology | establishes cosmological behavior from entangled CFT data | observable access is model dependent and not yet the sharpest singularity diagnostic | acceleration alone is not a singularity criterion |
| arXiv:2405.18465 | magnetic braneworld / BCFT strip model | encoded in a pure excited state dual to a black brane microstate | explicit flat big-bang/big-crunch braneworld cosmology | BCFT Euclidean path integral provides the cleanest microscopic anchor among the open-universe models | need to connect the BCFT data to a singularity-specific observable |
| arXiv:2507.10649 | closed universe / baby-universe holography | bulk entanglement controls direct encoding strength and no-entanglement breakdown | argues the CFT still determines closed-universe physics, even when direct access breaks down | final-state wavefunction dictionary and coarse-grained observables are explicit | strongest evidence for encoding; still not identical to geometric resolution |

## Working Diagnostic

The project will treat a paper as supporting singularity resolution only if it provides:

1. a boundary observable tied directly to the crunch region,
2. a reason that this observable is incompatible with an unresolved singularity,
3. a statement that survives the distinction between microscopic determination and observer access.

Most anchor papers currently satisfy item 1 partially, item 2 weakly, and item 3 only after careful interpretation.
